perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information ” means any information or data, of any kind or nature whatsoever , relating to or otherwise pertaining to a disclosing Party (the “Discloser ”) and/or any of the Discloser ’s subsidiaries, affiliates, agents, assigns or representatives, the Discloser ’s activities, employees, customers, subscribers, business, affairs , operations systems, software and any other information disclosed and/or otherwise made available to the other Party (the “ Recipient ”), in any form whatsoever , including any and all analyses, compilations, studies, reports or other documents prepared by the Discloser , or by its directors, officers, employees, advisers, affiliates, agents or representatives, all information relating to or otherwise pertaining to any trade secret, invention, idea, know -how, technical, financial and business information, business plans or processes , information provided by third parties to the Discloser under an obligation of confi dentiality , and all information which the Recipient has been exposed to in connection with the Subject Matter.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information ” means any tangible information or data, that is written or recorded and that is physically transferred between parties, relating to or otherwise pertaining to a disclosing Party (the “Discloser ”) and/or any of the Discloser ’s subsidiaries, affiliates, agents, assigns or representatives, the Discloser ’s activities, employees, customers, subscribers, business, affairs , operations systems, software and any other information disclosed and/or otherwise made available to the other Party (the “ Recipient ”), in any form whatsoever , including any and all analyses, compilations, studies, reports or other documents prepared by the Discloser , or by its directors, officers, employees, advisers, affiliates, agents or representatives, all information relating to or otherwise pertaining to any trade secret, invention, idea, know -how, technical, financial and business information, business plans or processes , information provided by third parties to the Discloser under an obligation of confi dentiality , and all information which the Recipient has been exposed to in connection with the Subject Matter.
perturbation.explanation: By specifying that 'Confidential Information' means *tangible* information that is *physically transferred*, the definition now *excludes* oral disclosures and data shared electronically or verbally. This *contradicts* later sections that assume a broader definition of 'Confidential Information', as it might include spoken discussions or access to digital databases, which are not physically transferred.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the Recipient , will not be disclosed to any person or entity in any way except as provided in this Agreement , and will be protected with the same degree of care the Recipient normally uses in the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no case with any less degree than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include only *tangible* information that is *physically transferred*. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states, 'All Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence'. If 'Confidential Information' is now limited to tangible, physically transferred information, then the obligation to maintain 'all' Confidential Information in confidence is undermined because it excludes other forms of information (e.g., oral disclosures). This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the scope of what must be kept confidential is narrowed to a specific tangible form, limiting the ability to protect other disclosed confidential information which conflicts with the general nature of the contradictory text.

perturbation.original_text: For greater certainty, all information exchanged between the Parties is to be considered Confidential Information, unless specifically stated otherwise . “Confidential Information ” will also include this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: For greater certainty, only written information exchanged between the Parties is to be considered Confidential Information, unless specifically stated otherwise . “Confidential Information ” will also include this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that *only written* information is 'Confidential Information', we directly *contradict* scenarios where information is exchanged verbally or electronically. This is a significant issue because the original text includes that all information exchanged between the parties is considered confidential, but the changed texts, removes it from the realm of all and only include the written information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Confidential Information may be disclosed by the Recipient only to those employees, directors, officers or consultants of the Recipient having the need to receive such Confidential Information for the Subject Matter , provided the recipients of such Confidential Information are already bound by wri tten confidentia lity and non -disclosure obligations similar to those undertaken by the Recipient pursuant to this Agreement . The Recipient shall immediately give notice to the Discloser of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information . The Recipient agrees to assist the Discloser in remedying any such unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all information exchanged is considered confidential. The changed text narrows this to only *written* information. The contradicted text discusses the disclosure of 'Confidential Information'. If only written information is confidential, then the contradicted text's discussion of disclosure is undermined when other forms of exchanged confidential information are not included. This creates a clear contradiction as oral exchanges of confidential information is no longer considered 'Confidential Information'. Therefore, this is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall immediately give notice to the Discloser of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information . The Recipient agrees to assist the Discloser in remedying any such unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall immediately give notice to the Discloser of any unauthorized use of the Confidential Information . The Recipient agrees to assist the Discloser in remedying any such unauthorized use of Confidential Information .
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'disclosure', the Recipient is now only required to notify the Discloser of unauthorized 'use' of Confidential Information. Unauthorized 'disclosure' is no longer explicitly covered, *creating a contradiction* because other sections imply that unauthorized disclosure is a breach. This creates a *contradiction* because it is implied that the Recipient must notify the Discloser of any unauthorized disclosure, but with the changed text, it doesn't explicitly say so.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Equitable Relief . The Recipient agrees that, in the event of any breach of any provision hereof, the Discloser may, or will, not have an adequate remedy in money or damages. The Recipient therefore agrees that, in such event, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against such breach in any court of competent jurisdiction. Such injunctive relief will in no way limit the Discloser’s right to obtain other remedies available at law or in equity. No failure or delay by the Discloser hereto i n enforcing any right, power or privilege created hereunder shall operate as an implied waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial enforcement thereof preclude any other or further enforcement thereof or the enforcement of any other right, power or pr ivilege.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'disclosure' from the Recipient's obligation to report unauthorized activity. The contradicted text discusses remedies for 'any breach', which would include unauthorized disclosure. Therefore, by removing the explicit mention of 'disclosure', the changed text creates a contradiction because the Recipient no longer has an explicit obligation to report unauthorized disclosures, which is an implied breach in the contradicted text, according to the Perturbation Explanation. This aligns with the 'Omissions' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will not use any Confidential Information for any purpose other th an the Subject Matter and will not use any Confidential Information for obtaining any intellectual property rights for the Recipient or to divert or attempt to divert any business, supplier or customer of the Discloser .
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will not use any Confidential Information for any purpose other th an the Subject Matter and will not use any Proprietary Information for obtaining any intellectual property rights for the Recipient or to divert or attempt to divert any business, supplier or customer of the Discloser .
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' mid-sentence, the Recipient is now allowed to use Confidential Information for obtaining any intellectual property rights, but NOT Proprietary Information. It makes it vague, since it is not clear if Proprietary and Confidential Information are the same, so this creates a *contradiction* because the initial clause restrict the Recipient to use the Confidential Information for any other propose other than the Subject matter, but the changed text, makes it vague, and even allows the Recipient to use the Confidential Information in any way that is not about obtaining any IP rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neit her this Agreement, nor either P arty’s performance under this Agreement, will transfer to the Recipient , or be construed to grant the Recipient , any proprietary right, license title, interest , licence or claim in or to any of the Confidential Information , other than the right to use the same strictly for the Subject Matter in accordance with the terms of this Agreement . The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Discloser is and shall remain the full and exclusive owner or licensee of the Confidential Information and the intellectual property rights thereto, including but not limited to derivatives, alterations, modifications and other changes thereto, whether made by the Discloser or the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the Recipient from using Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Subject Matter. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Information' in the phrase concerning intellectual property rights. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text because the changed text implies that the Recipient *can* use Confidential Information to obtain intellectual property rights, while the contradicted text explicitly states that no proprietary right, license, title, interest or claim in or to any of the Confidential Information will transfer to the Recipient , other than the right to use the same strictly for the Subject Matter.

perturbation.original_text: To the extent the Recipient is required to disclose any Confidential Information pursuant to a valid subpoena or other applicable order by a governmental agency or judicial body or by operation of law, the Recipient will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such disclosure so that the Discloser may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy from the proper authority.
perturbation.changed_text: To the extent the Recipient is required to disclose any Confidential Information pursuant to a valid subpoena or other applicable order by a governmental agency or judicial body or by operation of law, the Recipient will promptly notify the Discloser by email of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such disclosure so that the Discloser may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy from the proper authority.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mentions that the Recipient has to promptly notify the Discloser 'in writing', where as the modified text, specifies the channel of notification to 'by email', this might become a problem if the Recipient used another method of written communication, or if the recipient has not access to any email account, making it very hard to comply with the contract's obligations. This *contradicts* the general notification obligations, as a notification by email might not be the most efficient and secure form to do it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement . This Agreement consists of the entire agreement and understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior written or oral agreements with respect hereto. This Agreement may not be modified except by written instrument signed by a duly authorized representative of each party hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies notification 'in writing,' while the changed text limits this to 'by email.' The contradicted text states that the agreement may not be modified except by a written instrument signed by both parties. By specifying 'by email,' the changed text contradicts the requirement that modifications be made by 'written instrument,' because although an email can be considered written, it limits the definition of a 'written instrument' to only email notifications, thereby potentially excluding other forms of written communication and conflicting with the clause on modifications.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will commence upon the Effective Date and terminate on the expiry or termination of any engagement in relation to the Subject Matter between the Parties. Upon termination or expiry of this Agreement, the Recipient (i) will cease to use the C onfidential Information received from the Discloser, (ii) within thirty (30) days after the date of termination or expiry of this Agreement, will return to the Discloser all original Confidential Information thereof then in its possession or control, (iii) if the Discloser requests in writing, within thirty (30) days after the date of termination or expiry of this Agreement, will return to the Discloser all copies of the Confidential Information requested by the Discloser, (iv) if no request from the Disclo ser has been received within thirty (30) days of the termination or expiry of this agreement for the return of any copies of the Confidential Information, will destroy all copies thereof then in its possession or control and (v) upon the written request o f the Discloser, will furnish the Discloser with written certification of such destruction .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will commence upon the Effective Date and terminate on the expiry or termination of any engagement in relation to the Subject Matter between the Parties. Upon termination or expiry of this Agreement, the Recipient (i) may continue to use the C onfidential Information received from the Discloser, (ii) within thirty (30) days after the date of termination or expiry of this Agreement, will return to the Discloser all original Confidential Information thereof then in its possession or control, (iii) if the Discloser requests in writing, within thirty (30) days after the date of termination or expiry of this Agreement, will return to the Discloser all copies of the Confidential Information requested by the Discloser, (iv) if no request from the Disclo ser has been received within thirty (30) days of the termination or expiry of this agreement for the return of any copies of the Confidential Information, will destroy all copies thereof then in its possession or control and (v) upon the written request o f the Discloser, will furnish the Discloser with written certification of such destruction .
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will cease to use' to 'may continue to use' after termination introduces a direct *contradiction*. The original text strictly forbids using confidential information after the agreement ends, while the modified text allows it, creating ambiguity and uncertainty about the Recipient's obligations. This *contradicts* the overall purpose of the agreement, which is to protect the discloser's confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will not use any Confidential Information for any purpose other th an the Subject Matter and will not use any Confidential Information for obtaining any intellectual property rights for the Recipient or to divert or attempt to divert any business, supplier or customer of the Discloser .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original 'will cease to use' to 'may continue to use' the Confidential Information after termination. This directly contradicts the section that states 'The Recipient will not use any Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Subject Matter'. The original text strictly prohibited use after termination, while the changed text permits it. Therefore, the changed text creates a strong and undeniable contradiction within the document.
